name: The Ultimate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 450°F. Make the crust:
With a fork, toss graham cracker crumbs with butter and sugar until moistened. Transfer to a 9-inch springform pan. Evenly press onto pan, sides and bottom. Bake for 5 minutes or until golden. Cool.
Heat oven to 450°F.
Make the filling:
In a large bowl, beat cream cheese with sugar, flour, peels and vanilla until blended. Beat in eggs and yolks one at a time, scraping the bowl occasionally. Add heavy cream. Pour mixture into prepared pan.
Bake for 10 minutes. Lower temperature to 300°F. Bake for 1 hour and 10 minutes. The filling mixture will be loose but will set on standing. Cool cake on a wire rack for 2 hours.
With a spatula, spread sour cream over the top of the cake within 1/2 inch of the edge. Refrigerate for 3 hours or overnight.
Pare mango, slice flesh from pit and cut enough slices into small triangles to make 1/4 cup. Place remaining in a food processor. Puree until smooth. Pour into a small bowl.
Puree raspberries with their syrup. Pour through a sieve placed on a saucepan. Stir in cornstarch. Bring to boil, simmer for 1 minute (stirring) until thick and clear. Pour into a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate until cold.
Remove cake from pan, place on a serving dish. Spoon mango and raspberry sauces over the top of the cake. Garnish with mango, kiwifruit and fresh raspberries. Pass remaining fruit sauces.
